entities.description: reasoner operators are components that evaluate nodes using multiple dimensions such as graph topology and semantic relevance
skyline ranker is a method employed to obtain the final retrieval set by retaining the pareto frontier of nodes
the skyline operator is a mechanism that retains valuable nodes in at least one dimension while discarding dominated ones
n r represents the final retrieval set derived from the skyline ranker process
s g   n   s   is a function or metric used within the skyline ranker equation to evaluate nodes
t   n   is a function or metric used within the skyline ranker equation to evaluate nodes
n s represents the set of nodes from which the final retrieval set is derived
equation  14  defines the mathematical relationship for calculating the final retrieval set n r using the skyline ranker
graph topology is a dimension used by reasoner operators to evaluate nodes
semantic relevance is a dimension used by reasoner operators to evaluate nodes
the pareto frontier is the set of nodes retained by the skyline operator that are valuable in at least one dimension
fixed top  retrieval is a method contrasted with the skyline operator for its inability to retain the pareto frontier
noise is a factor minimized by the pre selection process to optimize foraging cost
foraging cost is the metric optimized by minimizing noise and focusing on relevant contexts
pre selection is a process that minimizes noise and ensures reasoning is applied only to highly relevant contexts

relations.description: reasoner operators evaluate nodes using multiple dimensions like graph topology and semantic relevance
the skyline ranker is employed to generate the final retrieval set
the skyline operator retains nodes that are valuable in at least one dimension and discards dominated ones
equation  14  mathematically defines the operation of the skyline ranker
n r is the output variable resulting from the skyline ranker operation
s g   n   s   is an input component used within the skyline ranker equation
t   n   is an input component used within the skyline ranker equation
n s is the set of nodes provided as input to the skyline ranker equation
reasoner operators use graph topology as a dimension for evaluation
reasoner operators use semantic relevance as a dimension for evaluation
the skyline operator retains the pareto frontier of nodes
the skyline operator is contrasted with fixed top  retrieval in the text
pre selection optimizes the foraging cost
n r represents the set of retained nodes
